latest.title: Placing to raise £500,000
latest.content:
16 Apr 2026 07:33
RNS Number : 7423A
Quantum Blockchain Technologies PLC
16 April 2026
16 April 2026
Quantum Blockchain Technologies Plc
(
"
QBT
" or
"
the Company
")
Placing to raise £500,000 and
New
Virtual Bitcoin Mining Subsidiary
Quantum Blockchain Technologies plc (AIM: QBT), the AIM-listed investment company focused principally on a research, development and investment programme within blockchain technology, is pleased to announce that it has raised £500,000 (before expenses) through the placing of 142,857,142 new ordinary shares of 0.25 pence each in the Company ("Placing Shares") at a price of 0.35 pence per Placing Share (the "Placing").
The net proceeds of the Placing will be used to allow the Company:
·
to fund its continued research and development programme for its proprietary technology aimed at improving the efficiency of bitcoin mining to allow the Company to start the
porting of the AI Oracle onto the mining rigs of new ASIC manufacturers.
·
to invest up to £100,000 into BlocKeeper, a new company which is developing a hardware-free Bitcoin "virtual mining" operation intended to selectively acquire hashing power from established Bitcoin miners and deploy hash rate to mine Bitcoins.
BlocKeeper
QBT has, over the past several months, been working on the formation of a new subsidiary company, BlocKeeper, whose strategy is to selectively acquire hashing power from established Bitcoin miners, providing these miners with liquidity at different stages in their mining cycles to cover cyclical or systemic funding shortfalls and providing BlocKeeper with the means to deploy hash rate to mine Bitcoins at competitive prices.
BlocKeeper will seek to harness QBT's extensive industry network, built up over the past five years, to establish itself within the Bitcoin market. It is further planned that BlocKeeper will utilise QBT's disruptive AI driven software products to improve the hashing performance of the mining companies with whom it is looking to establish commercial arrangements.
BlocKeeper will be appointing an experienced board of directors, including Alessandro Malacart, as CEO. Mr Malacart brings 12 years' experience in the digital start-up investment sector. Two independent non-executive directors have also agreed to join the board, subject to completion of director due diligence; one of whom is a former co-head of quantitative analysis at a major US investment bank in New York, while the second is currently the treasurer of a New York Stock Exchange listed international industrial manufacturing company.
QBT will continue to focus on its core R&D programme where it is looking to deliver its Bitcoin mining software products to the market. Its business activities and board of directors will remain separate to those of BlocKeeper, except in areas of business where synergies are available to the benefit of both companies.
The Company is seeking to list BlocKeeper on AQSE Stock Exchange Growth Market. Nevertheless, QBT shareholders should note that there remain several contingent aspects of this process. Accordingly, there can be no certainty that the proposed listing will occur.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application will be made for the Placing Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM with admission expected to occur at 8.00a.m. on or around 21 April 2026. The Placing Shares will rank pari passu with the Company's existing ordinary shares.
Following admission of the Placing Shares, the Company's issued share capital will comprise 1,608,083,941 ordinary shares of 0.25 pence each. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.
Francesco Gardin, CEO and Executive Chairman of QBT, commented
:
"Today's placing allows us to start the porting of our technology onto the mining rigs of new ASIC manufacturers.
"The funding also provides initial seed capital for the launch of our new venture, BlocKeeper. We have been looking at ways for some time now to create a valid business strategy to physically mine Bitcoin utilising our extensive industry network and own cutting-edge technology. We have developed BlocKeeper to complement our core strategy of deploying our AI-based software products to Bitcoin miners. BlocKeeper is, we believe, a unique and scaleable way to enter this market.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (
"
MAR"
), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company
'
s obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
- ends -
For further information please contact:
Quantum Blockchain Technologies Plc
+39 335 296573
Francesco Gardin, CEO and Executive Chairman
SP Angel Corporate Finance
(Nominated Adviser & Broker) +44 (0) 20 3470 0470
Caroline Rowe / Devik Mehta
Leander
(Financial PR) +44 (0) 7795 168 157
Christian Taylor-Wilkinson
About Quantum Blockchain Technologies Plc
QBT (AIM: QBT) is a London Stock Exchange AIM listed Research & Development and investing company focused on an intensive R&D programme to disrupt the Blockchain Technologies sector which includes, cryptocurrency mining and other advanced blockchain applications. The primary goal of the R&D programme is to develop Bitcoin mining tools and techniques, via its technology-driven approach, which the Company believes will significantly outperform existing market practices.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: QBT Ltd runs a small‑scale Bitcoin mining operation that also develops AI‑assisted mining software. They sell and lease mining rigs and offer cloud‑based mining services to retail and institutional users.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Bitcoin mining remains a key part of the cryptocurrency ecosystem, and companies that can lower energy costs or improve hash‑rate efficiency are highly sought after. QBT’s focus on AI‑optimised rigs and patent‑protected software gives it a niche advantage in a market where hardware upgrades happen every few months.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in a growth phase, rolling out new rigs and refining its AI platform. Cash burn is moderate, but recent bond meetings and patent filings suggest management is actively securing capital and protecting intellectual property.

rns_narrative: The latest announcements show QBT moving from simply delivering mining rigs to adding an AI‑driven oracle that can optimise mining parameters in real time. This tech upgrade is paired with a series of patent updates, indicating a push to defend its competitive edge.

Bondholder meetings and funding updates point to a strategic effort to shore up liquidity, while the delivery of new rigs demonstrates operational momentum. The company’s recent conference update suggests it is positioning itself for a larger market share in the mining‑as‑a‑service space.
Overall, the company appears to be stabilising after a period of volatility, with new tech and capital raising forming the core of its recovery narrative.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – traders are waiting for the AI upgrade to pay off.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls argue that the AI Oracle will boost hash‑rate efficiency, reducing operating costs and creating a clear value‑add for customers. They see the recent rig deliveries and patent filings as proof that QBT is on a path to scale.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s business model still relies on volatile Bitcoin prices and that the AI platform may not deliver the promised gains. They point to the lack of recent revenue data and the heavy reliance on debt as potential red flags.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from skeptical to cautiously hopeful since the AI announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for QBT is flat, indicating a lack of public hype. This silence means any positive catalyst could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Official launch of the AI Oracle in the next 2–3 weeks – a clear signal that the technology is ready for market.
Positive earnings or cash‑flow numbers from the new rigs, proving the upgrade translates to higher profitability.
Any announcement of a partnership or bulk rig order from a large mining pool, which would confirm institutional interest.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 20‑day average by 30% or more, indicating renewed buying pressure.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the 20‑day moving average for a clear break above the recent low. Watch for a 3‑day volume surge coupled with a 1‑day price rise of at least 5%. Any sign that the AI platform has been integrated into a new customer contract is a positive confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is likely in the early stages of a recovery from a deep sell‑off. The APEX compression score suggests the price is still under pressure, so a breakout from the current support zone is needed to confirm mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the AI Oracle proves effective and the company secures a few large rig orders, the stock could rebound from its current low to the mid‑range of its 52‑week band. A 50% upside would be realistic if the price reaches the midpoint of the recent low‑high swing, offering a 2:1 reward to risk.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the AI upgrade fails to deliver cost savings, or that Bitcoin price stays flat, keeping operating costs high. A worst‑case scenario is a further 10–15% decline if the market rejects the new technology and the company’s debt burden becomes unsustainable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~50% from current lows. Potential downside: ~10–15% if the product stalls. Risk‑reward ratio: roughly 3:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed breakout above the 20‑day moving average and a volume spike of at least 30% above the 20‑day average. Allocate 10–12% of the portfolio to the position, with a stop 12% below the entry to protect against a short‑term pullback.
playbook.if_watching: Set a price alert at 2% above the current low. If the stock closes above this level on a volume spike, consider a partial entry. Also watch for the AI Oracle launch date; a positive press release could justify a full entry.
playbook.if_passing: There are no immediate terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy. If the company announces a default on its bonds or fails to meet a scheduled payment, consider exiting or tightening stops.

ai_insights.bottom_line: QBT’s deep crash and silent market make it a contrarian play; a successful AI Oracle rollout and a few key rig sales could lift the stock to mid‑range levels, offering a 3:1 upside if the company’s tech delivers.
